title: 'Tea laced with sleeping pills': Hunt for 32 missing police guns uncovers murder plot
content: A shocking double life has been exposed in Faridabad, where a 22-year-old ITI trainee, Monu, arrested for stealing 32 guns from a police station, has confessed to strangling his cousin last July. He and his girlfriend, Sarita, allegedly drugged and then smothered Shivam before dumping his body in a canal. Divers are now searching the canal for the victim's remains.

title: 'Why should we not kill the man...?' Calls to assassinate Trump at Khamenei's funeral
content: Chants of 'Death to America!' echoed at Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral as a performer called for Donald Trump's demise. Hundreds of thousands mourned the late Supreme Leader, displaying anti-US and anti-Israel sentiment. This comes amid ongoing negotiations with Washington, highlighting deep-seated animosity. The funeral, delayed due to recent conflict, also saw prayers led by Ayatollah Jafar Sobhani, with key Iranian officials in attendance.

title: 'Spare a thought for Samson': Ex-cricketer reacts to India dropping World Cup hero
content: Ambati Rayudu defended Sanju Samson after his T20I exclusion, reminding fans of Samson's T20 World Cup Player of the Tournament award.  While welcoming 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's debut, Rayudu emphasized Samson's recent match-winning contributions. Samson's omission follows a single low score, sparking debate despite Sooryavanshi's promising, albeit brief, innings. England leads the series after a victory.

title: As Mamata takes charge after Chandrima's resignation, BJP says 'TMC no longer exists'
content: West Bengal minister Dilip Ghosh claimed the Trinamool Congress is facing a "wave of resignations" and is effectively defunct, following Chandrima Bhattacharya's exit from all party posts. Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has reassumed leadership amidst internal turmoil and alleged BJP intimidation. Ghosh questioned Bhattacharya's tenure and Banerjee's budget preparation methods, while Bhattacharya cited hurt over Banerjee's accusations regarding Trinamool Bhavan's control.

title: As riding costs hurt, Tesla tells employees: You cannot spend more than $200 on…
content: Tesla is capping employee AI spending at $200 a week from July 6, according to an internal memo. The move reverses months of Tesla pushing staff to use AI harder, after some engineers burned thousands in tokens weekly. The cap notably exempts Elon Musk's xAI beta products, steering workers toward Grok and Composer—even as many Tesla engineers quietly prefer Anthropic's Claude.

title: UK, Japan and Italy pump $6.1 billion into sixth-generation GCAP fighter
content: Edgewing, a collaborative venture formed by BAE Systems, Leonardo, and JAIEC, has secured a remarkable £4.6 billion contract to spearhead the development of a state-of-the-art sixth-generation fighter jet for the UK, Italy, and Japan. This investment ignites the next stage of the Global Combat Air Programme (GCAP), striving for operational capability by 2035.

title: 'No engine damage but mileage drops': Carmakers asked to send out ‘safe E20’ message
content: Vehicle manufacturers are defending the government's mandatory 20% ethanol blending in petrol, assuring the public that no engine damage has been observed. While acknowledging a slight drop in fuel efficiency for older vehicles due to lower calorific value, industry leaders state field experience has been positive. Discussions are ongoing with the government regarding flex-fuel vehicles as a future pathway.

title: Watch: Mbappe ignores handshake before Paraguay goalkeeper loses cool
content: Kylian Mbappe scored the decisive penalty as France national football team beat Paraguay national football team 1-0 to reach the World Cup quarter-finals. The match ended in controversy after Paraguay goalkeeper Orlando Gill threw the ball at Mbappe following an apparent handshake snub. Mbappe later said: "We can also get our hands dirty. We know how to play ugly football."

title: Sergey Brin sold his NYC real estate: Here's what Mamdani's rent freeze cost him
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin has divested his stake in a New York City real estate fund for a fraction of its value, selling for just six cents on the dollar. This significant loss comes as Mayor Zohran Mamdani's rent freeze tightens conditions for landlords. The fund, A&E Real Estate, confirmed the sale, citing the challenging market for rent-stabilized apartments.

title: Chants, tears & prayers: Massive crowd gathers in Tehran for Ali Khamenei funeral - watch
content: Millions of Iranians are participating in extensive funeral ceremonies for Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei in Tehran, following his death in an airstrike. The Grand Mosalla complex is overwhelmed with mourners paying respects, with prayers held for Khamenei and family members killed alongside him. Chants of "Death to America!" and "Death to Israel!" echoed, reflecting regional tensions. The ongoing events are being closely monitored amid a sensitive geopolitical climate.

title: After 8,000 layoffs, Zuckerberg admits Meta misjudged AI progress
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg acknowledged internal restructuring hasn't yielded expected results, particularly in AI agent development, admitting executives 'miscalculated' timelines. Despite significant layoffs and AI investments, progress has been slower than anticipated. However, Zuckerberg anticipates substantial AI benefits within three to six months. Separately, the company addressed mouse-tracking software, assuring no employee data was used for AI training and confirming future opt-in policies.

title: '53 marks in JEE': Student shares how he still got into Stanford, Princeton & Caltech
content: A Stanford student's surprisingly low JEE score of 15% contrasted with his admission to Caltech, Princeton, and Stanford highlights India's intense engineering competition.  While US universities consider diverse factors, the JEE primarily tests exam performance.  This disparity underscores the vast talent pool in India, with the student emphasizing the scale of technical competition over individual academic journeys.

title: When and how can landlords evict tenants? What the Model Tenancy Act says
content: The Model Tenancy Act, 2021 introduces significant reforms allowing landlords to remove troublesome tenants through legal channels. It specifies clear eviction grounds, such as non-payment of rent and unauthorized subleasing. Eviction proceedings must take place in the Rent Court, eliminating unlawful forceful evictions. Tenants who resist vacating risk severe penalties, including paying double or quadruple rent. Furthermore, legal heirs can also request eviction based on genuine needs.

title: ‘Why Indian women could play football World Cup before men’
content: Aditi Chauhan, initially a basketball enthusiast, transitioned to football as a goalkeeper, becoming India's number one and the first Indian woman to play in the English league. Despite facing career-threatening ACL injuries and the absence of a professional league, Chauhan pursued sports management and returned to play for India.

title: After desertions, Mamata Banerjee formally takes over as TMC state chief
content: Mamata Banerjee has reassumed leadership of her party amidst an organizational crisis, accusing the BJP of engineering defections through intimidation. She declared her Kolkata residence the party's permanent office, asserting that supporters, not symbols or buildings, define Trinamool's identity. Banerjee vowed to fight political persecution and warned rebels against aligning with the BJP while claiming party affiliation.

title: 'Sabse pehle maine ... ': Sooryavanshi's first reaction after getting India cap
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi etched his name in history, becoming India's youngest international cricketer at 15 years and 99 days, surpassing Sachin Tendulkar's long-standing record. Receiving his debut cap at Old Trafford, the left-handed opener shared an emotional moment with his father and delivered a fearless 14-run knock, announcing his arrival on the global stage with two impressive sixes.

title: 'Snapchat' clue in Ketan's murder: Days after Siya blamed Chetan, her 'text' adds twist
content: Days after blaming each other for the murder of fiancé Ketan Agarwal, police recovered a Snapchat message from accused Siya Goyal expressing doubts about her upcoming wedding. The message, sent weeks before the crime, suggests the marriage was "anyway not going to take place." Investigators are verifying the message and questioning the recipient, as digital evidence and witness statements are being analyzed to unravel the conspiracy.

title: 'We're heroic people with heroic spirit': Trump marks America's 250th Independence Day
content: US President Donald Trump marked America's 250th independence anniversary with a patriotic speech on the National Mall, celebrating the nation's enduring spirit and strength. Despite severe storms briefly disrupting the event and forcing an evacuation, thousands gathered for the occasion. Trump lauded veterans and the country's founding principles, while also weaving in partisan themes ahead of upcoming elections. Celebrations across the US were impacted by extreme weather.

title: American nightmare: Beneath glossy stories of Indian diaspora lies a darker reality
content: Justice remains elusive for Indian students abroad, with cases like Nikita Godishala's stalled and prime accused absconding. A troubling trend reveals 141 Indian student deaths in the US between 2021-2025 from various causes. Mohammed Abdul Arfath's perplexing death, following a ransom call and his body found in Lake Erie, highlights unresolved investigations and unanswered questions plaguing grieving families.

title: Ravi Bishnoi registers unwanted T20I record after becoming first spinner to...
content: Ravi Bishnoi etched an unwanted record, becoming the first spinner from a Full Member nation to bowl three no-balls in a men's T20I, significantly impacting India's loss to England. Despite a competitive total and a debut for 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, Bishnoi's expensive spell, including a 29-run over, proved decisive. England chased down the target with an over to spare, taking a 1-0 series lead.

title: ‘Capital punishment warranted’: Ram Mandir Trust member on Ayodhya donation row
content: A Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust member has endorsed the administration's decisive action in the alleged Ram Mandir donation embezzlement case, calling for severe penalties for the guilty. The ongoing SIT probe has led to arrests and resignations, with investigators scrutinizing financial irregularities and alleged siphoning of funds. Despite the probe, temple worship continues smoothly, and donation handling is reportedly now fully transparent.

title: Anshula Kapoor wedding: Janhvi and Khushi Kapoor to host mehendi ceremony - details
content: Ahead of her July 6 wedding to Rohan Thakkar, sisters Janhvi and Khushi Kapoor have secretly planned an intimate mehendi celebration

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi makes history at 15, but why isn't he the youngest ever?
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi made history on Saturday, becoming India's youngest international cricketer and the second-youngest debutant among Full Member nations. He achieved this milestone at 15 years and 99 days during the second T20I against England. Despite a brief but impactful 14 off 10 balls, including two sixes, India ultimately fell short in the match.

title: Houses, SUVs & more: How lifestyles of Ram temple theft accused changed dramatically
content: An investigation into Ram Temple donations in Ayodhya has revealed a stark financial uplift among accused individuals, with some building homes and acquiring SUVs despite modest known incomes. Evidence suggests significant daily siphoning of funds, estimated at Rs 6 to Rs 8 lakh, particularly during the Maha Kumbh period. Concerns were raised about the handling of precious metal donations and the lack of proper oversight.

title: Who is eligible for Delhi’s slum rehabilitation that will help 20 lakh people
content: Delhi's innovative Slum and JJ Cluster Rehabilitation and Relocation Policy, 2026, aims to transform the lives of approximately 20 lakh residents by providing them with permanent housing solutions. Emphasizing the idea of 'Jahan Jhuggi Wahan Makan', the policy focuses on in-situ rehabilitation, ensuring access to essential amenities. To qualify, JJ clusters must be established by January 1, 2025, with specific documentation requirements.

title: Gaganyaan mission: Isro's parachute test vehicle clears first ground test
content: India's space agency, ISRO, has successfully tested the solid motor for its new SOLVE launch vehicle, a crucial step for the Gaganyaan human spaceflight program. This test platform will aid in validating the crew module's parachute system for safe return.  SOLVE, adapted from PSLV technology, will offer flexibility in simulating mission conditions, paving the way for upcoming uncrewed Gaganyaan flights.

title: 'Paraguay's players were absolute disgrace': South American side slammed after WC exit
content: France reached the quarter-finals with a tense 1-0 win over France national football team, but the match was overshadowed by the behaviour of Paraguay national football team. Joe Hart called Paraguay's players "an absolute disgrace", while Micah Richards described their conduct as "embarrassing". Kylian Mbappe scored the winning penalty to set up a quarter-final clash with Morocco.

title: 'Watching you': Rs 200 fine for littering or spitting at stations, on trains
content: Eastern Railway has launched a strict cleanliness drive, "Eastern Railway is Watching You," imposing a Rs 200 fine for littering and spitting. Commuters can report offenders by sending photos to a dedicated WhatsApp number, 9002022780.  CCTV surveillance will also be used to catch those defacing railway premises. Passengers reporting offenders will be recognized as "Captain Clean."

title: 'Unwell since his death': Ketan Agarwal's grandfather dies of cardiac arrest
content: The grandfather of Ketan Agarwal, a Pune realtor whose death at Lohagad Fort is being investigated as murder, has passed away weeks after his grandson's demise. Devichand Agarwal reportedly suffered a cardiac arrest following the shock. Police are investigating the alleged murder of Ketan Agarwal, with his fiancée, Siya Goyal, and her alleged lover arrested. A Snapchat message from Goyal, expressing doubts about the wedding, has surfaced.

title: British GP 2026: Antonelli claims pole after sprint win, Hamilton misses out
content: Silverstone witnessed a thrilling start to the British Grand Prix weekend, with Mercedes rookie Kimi Antonelli securing pole position for Sunday's race. Despite Lewis Hamilton's strong performance, topping practice and taking Sprint pole, Antonelli fought back to win the Sprint race and then claimed his fifth pole of the season. Ferrari's Leclerc and Hamilton will start second and third, setting up an exciting Mercedes-Ferrari battle.

title: Padma Vibhushan Teejan Bai passes away at 70; tributes pour in for Pandavani legend
content: India mourns the loss of Padma Vibhushan Teejan Bai, a legendary Pandavani artist, who passed away at 69 after a prolonged illness. Hospitalized for breathing difficulties and weakness, her condition deteriorated at AIIMS, Raipur. Bai, a pioneer of the Mahabharata storytelling tradition, was celebrated for her dedication to preserving folk art, earning numerous prestigious awards throughout her illustrious career.

title: Watch: New York's Brooklyn Bridge catches fire during July 4 fireworks display
content: During Macy's Fourth of July fireworks extravaganza, the stunning Brooklyn Bridge briefly caught fire, sending flames and dark smoke billowing up as fireworks lit up the night sky above. Eyewitnesses reported that the fire began modestly but intensified quickly, with additional explosions reportedly triggered by the blaze. The dramatic scene unfolded over approximately one minute, leaving uncertainty about any potential damage to the historic structure.

title: Kim Jong Un oversees nuclear-capable missile tests from newly repaired warship
content: North Korea's Kim Jong Un has overseen tests of nuclear-capable cruise missiles and other systems aboard a newly repaired 5,000-ton destroyer, the Kang Kon. This move signals Pyongyang's intensified efforts to bolster its naval nuclear capabilities, following the recent commissioning of another similar vessel. Kim has ordered the Kang Kon to be operational within two months, underscoring a strategic shift towards expanding its sea-based nuclear arsenal.

title: NFL Trade Rumor: Jets, Dolphins top landing spots for Browns' $4m rookie
content: The Cleveland Browns’ star player, Shedeur Sanders, has found his name regularly in trade rumors after his impressive performance in the last season, sparking chaos about his future. Ahead of the new season, it seems the New York Jets and the Miami Dolphins have emerged as potential landing spots for him.

title: France vs Paraguay becomes one of hottest World Cup matches ever
content: France lead Paraguay in a FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 16 clash played in extreme heat at Philadelphia’s Lincoln Financial Field, with temperatures near 100°F (37.8°C) and heat index reaching 105–110°F (40.6–43.3°C). The extreme conditions, under an official heat warning, have turned the match into a severe physical test, with Kylian Mbappé’s penalty separating the sides.

title: Why Delhi court denied bail to Umar Khalid, Sharjeel Imam again
content: A Delhi court has rejected the bail pleas of Umar Khalid and Sharjeel Imam in the 2020 riots larger conspiracy case. The judge stated the court must adhere to a Supreme Court ruling from January 5, 2026, which had previously dismissed their bail petitions. The court found no grounds to reconsider the matter, citing the apex court's prior decision.

title: 'He's not over her': Taylor Swift's ex boyfriend Matty Healy sparks fresh speculation
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding has sparked chaos across the world and especially in New York City as billions wait for some wedding pictures to be leaked after they got married last night. Away from the chaos in New York City, Taylor Swift’s ex boyfriend, Matty Healy’s outing last evening has convinced many that he is not over her.

title: Serena Williams vs Venus Williams net worth comparison: Which sister is richer
content: Serena Williams and Venus Williams are more than just tennis legends. They are also smart businesswomen. Both sisters earned millions during their playing careers. But they did not stop there. They turned their success into businesses! They made smart investments, and got huge paydays through brand deals.

title: 'Irreplaceable loss to art & culture': PM Modi mourns demise of folk singer Teejan Bai
content: Renowned Pandavani singer Teejan Bai has passed away, leaving an irreplaceable void in the world of art and culture. Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Chhattisgarh Chief Minister Vishnu Deo Sai paid glowing tributes, highlighting her immense contribution to popularizing the traditional folk art form globally. Her powerful performances, rooted in the Mahabharata, earned her prestigious national honours and brought immense pride to her state.

title: All allies united, NDA to focus on repeating 2017 poll win: BJP leaders after meeting Nitin Nabin
content: Leaders of the BJP allies in the State said the meeting would focus on strengthening the coalition, expanding its organisational base and evolving a strategy to ensure the NDA's return to power in the State

title: Ram temple donation row: VHP asks SIT to verify claims made by Opposition leaders
content: VHP said that the statements of Opposition leaders will further help the investigative agencies to probe the allegations on Ram Temple donation theft

title: How does WhatsApp’s proposed username feature compare to Telegram’s? | Explained
content: Government says the feature could ‘facilitate impersonation and identity spoofing, including impersonation by permitting the adoption of usernames closely resembling those of genuine persons or institutions’

title: Gaganyaan mission: ISRO successfully conducts first SOLVE ground test
content: ISRO is developing a solid motor based SOLVE as a test platform to carry out Integrated Parachute Tests for validation of deceleration system of Gaganyaan’s Crew Module under various test conditions.

title: Monsoon tracker July 5 LIVE: Mumbai wakes up to brief respite after heavy rain; IMD issues red alert for more showers
content: Delhi weather department has predicted a cloudy sky with moderate rain, and the maximum temperature is expected to settle at 36 degrees Celsius

title: Pune building's compound wall collapses after rains; 14 vehicles damaged
content: "The residential society is located on Jambhulwadi Road in Katraj. The compound wall of the society collapsed onto the parking shed following incessant heavy rainfall. Several vehicles, including two-wheelers and four-wheelers, were damaged," a fire official said

title: Two Kashmir Shia clerics ‘disallowed’ to travel to Iran for Ali Khamenei’s final rites
content: J&K Anjuman-e-Sharie Shian president Aga Syed Hassan Al-Mosavi Al-Safavi was stopped by Bureau of Immigration; Shia cleric Masroor Abbas Ansari was also “denied a passport”

title: Government withdraws emergency gas curbs imposed during Hormuz disruption as LNG supplies normalise
content: The Ministry said the situation has since improved, with a ceasefire in place, negotiations underway and maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz resuming.

title: On RJD’s 30th foundation day, Lalu Prasad says constitutional institution compromised
content: He also alleged that the BJP is attempting to push the country backward through authoritarian means by capturing constitutional institutions and sidelining the constitution itself.

title: Tribal children in Maharashtra send postcards to CM seeking free education for kids up to 18
content: The children wrote postcards to convey their message to Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis as part of an awareness campaign organised by a tribal rights organisation, said officials of the organisation

title: Another batch of pilgrims on way to Amarnath shrine
content: The batch, including 1,310 women and 22 children, left the Jammu base camp in two separate convoys at 3 a.m. and 3.40 a.m., the officials said.

title: Bring Ram Janmabhoomi Trust within RTI purview: CPI(M) leader John Brittas
content: CPI(M) Rajya Sabha leader says trust was constituted through government action and should be subject to transparency provisions under the RTI Act

title: Telangana SIR: Political parties save the day as BLOs navigate unfamiliar territories to decide electors’ fate
content: In majority of the areas, the BLOs are clueless about the areas and houses to be covered; drawn from several departments, they are unable to understand the vague maps given to them on the mobile app

title: Pandavani legend Teejan Bai dies at 70
content: Teejan Bai was widely regarded as the foremost exponent of Pandavani, the traditional folk art form of Chhattisgarh that narrates episodes from the Indian epic Mahabharata

title: Six fishermen missing after boat capsizes off Visakhapatnam coast
content: "One fisherman has been rescued by a cruise ship, while search operations are underway for the remaining six," Vizag Fishing Boats Association president Lakshman Rao told PTI

title: Stale food, cockroaches, rat droppings found at Hyderabad restaurants during food safety inspections
content: Officials noted an inadequate utensil washing area where hot water sanitisation was not being followed, along with flaking walls and ceilings in the vegetable cutting section

title: ‘One Nation, One Election initiative would fracture country’s federal compact’
content: Conclave on ‘One-Nation One-Election, Federalism and Citizenship’ also raises concern on SIR, citizenship and ECI functioning

title: News in Frames: Fastest hooves first on the track
content: Widely regarded as one of Maharashtra’s most prestigious bullock cart races, the Hindkesari Bailgada Sharyat once again drew a massive turnout in Pedgaon

title: Operative behind recce of Ram temple and RSS HQ, Hafiz Saeed's relative in designated terrorist list
content: All 23 of them were designated as terrorist under fourth schedule of the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act (UAPA)

title: Search for two LeT terrorists enters second day in J&K's Shopian
content: The searches have been resumed to track down the two terrorists who were spotted by surveillance cameras on Friday (July 3, 2026) in an orchard in the Meemandar area, which comprises seven villages

title: Eknath Shinde admitted to hospital, condition stable
content: Doctors attributed the ailment to a combination of viral fever and physical exhaustion from sustained activity

title: BJP will register a decisive victory in U.P., says party chief Nitin Nabin
content: Mr. Nabin, who reached Lucknow on a two-day visit, added that he was overwhelmed by the respect received in the land of Lord Shri Ram.

title: EAM Jaishankar to embark on 6-nation tour from July 5
content: External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar's visit to the Gulf nations comes against the backdrop of fast-evolving political dynamics in the region following the peace agreement between the U.S. and  Iran

title: Finance Ministry notifies rules for determination of origin of goods under India-U.K. trade pact
content: CETA secures duty-free access for 99% of India’s exports to the U.K., covering nearly the entire trade basket

title: Delhi court rejects Umar Khalid, Sharjeel Imam bail pleas, says bound by Supreme Court order
content: Trial court holds that it has ‘no option’ but to follow the Supreme Court’s January order; defence relies on the top court’s subsequent ruling in Syed Iftikhar Andrabi v. NIA which it said reaffirmed the principle that prolonged incarceration could justify bail

title: Hyderabad Police seize 122 tonnes of adulterated food, book 185 cases in H-FAST’s first 100 days
content: Rajendranagar recorded the highest number of cases at 55, followed by Golconda (44), Secunderabad (35), Charminar (21), Shamshabad (13), Jubilee Hills (12) and Khairatabad (5)

title: India weathered West Asia crisis through strategic decision-making, diplomacy: PM Modi
content: India avoided fuel rationing, LPG disruptions, price surges, with domestic refineries upping their LPG production and public sector OMCs bearing the losses, says PM; promises further expansion of refinery capacity

title: Railway ministry postpones formal launch of bullet train tunneling work amid Mumbai rains
content: Ministry officials said it has been raining in Mumbai for the past few days, and IMD has issued 'red' and 'orange' alerts for the next couple of days

title: Bihar to set up 100 fast track courts
content: The Chief Minister asserted that the state’s judiciary, police, and administration bear the major responsibility in delivering justice to a population of over 14 crores representing approximately 10% of the country’s total population.

title: Roadside vendors turned exporters: Meghalaya’s ‘numbered’ farmers reap fruits of labour
content: A 433-member organic farmers’ producer company in Ri-Bhoi district’s Jirang has become a model of successful farm collectivisation

title: Experts emphasise E20 compliant, thoroughly tested amid raging concerns
content: It said as per its service protocol, the fuel tank and fuel lines were drained, thoroughly cleaned and the vehicle was refilled with Standard E20 fuel.

title: Fresh political support pours in on 15th day of CJP protest; student hospitalised
content: Activist Sonam Wangchuk continues seventh day of his hunger strike; political leaders say will raise the issue of irregularities in examinations during Parliament’s upcoming Monsoon Session

title: NDPS cases, arrests double in Kochi in a month
content: Of the 1,143 drug-related cases registered in the first six months of the year, 503 were recorded in June alone following the launch of the new anti-drug operation

title: Ninth edition of The Hindu Home Expo commences at LuLu Mall
content: Mayor hails the expo as ‘a bridge to Keralite’s housing dreams’. Among the key highlights of the event are special rate offers from developers, reduced bank interest rates, and on-the-spot interactions with property developers

title: Traffic snarls worsen around Kochi metro extension, NHAI flyover worksites
content: Police say unscientific site management, potholes and inadequate supervision at the ongoing works have worsened traffic snarls on key arterial roads

title: J&K L-G administration suspends 8 officials over pro-separatist content in library books
content: An official spokesman said the book, “Personalities and Legends of J&K”, contained content related to separatism, which has the potential to create law and order situations

title: Two DMK MLAs were offered Ministry post, money to join TVK, alleges R.S. Bharathi
content: A delegation from the DMK met Governor Rajendra Viswanath Arlekar and submitted a memorandum seeking action into allegations of ‘horse-trading’ by the ruling party and over the alleged participation of two private individuals in a Cabinet meeting

title: Prepare contingency plan for sowing amid delayed monsoon: Official
content: Officials told to ensure adequate stocks of seeds and fertilizers and prepare for drinking water scarcity if dry spell continues.

title: Install aerators, use drinking water judiciously, BWSSB tells citizens
content: Given a severely deficit monsoon this year, with water levels in reservoirs already running low, the summer of 2027 is expected to be parched

title: AIADMK complains to Arlekar over ‘inaction’ on its petitions on ‘horse trading’
content: Speaking to reports after meeting Arlekar, the party’s agricultural wing secretary and whip in the Assembly ‘Agri’ S.S. Krishnamurthy said there was no follow up to his party’s memorandum given to Assembly Speaker J.C.D. Prabhakar

title: It’s time for Tamil Nadu to segregate solid waste at source
content: Though the new Solid Waste Management Rules kicked in on April 1, 2026, there has been little awareness among residents of this momentous change in urban solid waste management. While most parts of the world have settled into a routine of segregation of waste at source, Tamil Nadu still packs a mixed garbage bag. This report looks at how the new Rules could impact source segregation in the State

title: 60% of ammonia gas cleared from seafood factory in Tiruvallur
content: The operation was undertaken by the district administration and a team of experts; work to remove the remaining gas will resume today

title: Monsoon tracker July 4 Highlights: IMD issues red alert in five districts in Odisha
content: Incessant and heavy rains threw life out of gear in Mumbai and its metropolitan region; the monsoon is active in Gujarat with “exceptional heavy rainfall” in Junagadh, where a few areas witnessed severe waterlogging

title: Maharashtra TET scam: Three more persons arrested
content: Ten people have been arrested in the case so far; they have been remanded to police custody till July 9

title: After Tariff Relief, Belgian Firm Gifts Trump Ring With 321 Diamonds, 6 Rubies
content: All told, 321 diamonds, 56 sapphires, 13 emeralds and six rubies encrust the watch-sized gold ring presented this week to Bill White, the US ambassador to Belgium, to give to US President Donald Trump.

title: Cops Trace Sources Of Rs 15 Lakh Transactions In Ram Temple Theft Case
content: An FIR was registered on June 25 after the SIT submitted the preliminary report on June 23, and eight accused were arrested

title: "Quit Immediately": Pakistan Deputy PM Faces Flak After Grandson Arrested For Rape
content: Senator Faisal Vawda also accused the federal government and the provincial government in Punjab of protecting the minister's relative in the high-profile case.

title: Noida Woman Books E-Rickshaw, Goes To Multi-Level Parking, Jumps To Death
content: The incident took place on Saturday when the woman, identified as Situ Talwar, hired an e-rickshaw to reach the parking facility.

title: 2 Lawrence Bishnoi Gang Members With Rs 1 Lakh Reward Killed In Encounter
content: Police said both men were wanted in connection with the sensational daylight murder of a gym operator, Kapil, in Hansi on June 11.

title: 'Death To America, Death To Israel' Chants Echo At Khamenei's Funeral In Iran
content: The comment represents the first, direct call for Trump's death by an emcee at the funeral, which has seen posters and graffiti calling for the killing of Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.

title: Dollar Bills With Trump's Signature Rolls Out As US Marks 250 Years
content: Traditionally, US paper currency contains the signature of the treasury secretary and the US treasurer, not the president.

title: Publisher Whose Book Called Terrorists "Great Personalities" Raided In J&K
content: Apart from sections under the BNS, the FIR included charges under the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act or UAPA. After filing the case, the police raided one of the premises of the publishers in Jammu

title: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei Funeral News Updates: Thousands Gather In Iran As Funeral Rites For Khamenei Enter Second Day
content: Ali Khamenei's Last Rites News Live: Sunday has been declared a public holiday for the entire country, and in the evening, Khamenei's body will leave the Grand Mosalla complex

title: Telangana Man Robs Woman Of Gold, Cash, Pushes Her Into Well. She Survives For 21 Hours
content: Police said the case initially began as a missing person complaint after 55-year-old Tangarala Lakshmi, a daily wage worker, failed to return home on July 1.

title: 'Displayed For Months, Then Removed': Ex Bureaucrat To NDTV On His Gift To Ram Temple
content: The former Home Secretary said that the gold-plated 'Ramcharitmanas' was not only a gift, but it was the end result of a long spiritual journey that his family underwent.

title: Army Plugs Escape Routes At J&K Orchard Where Camera Detected 2 Lashkar Terrorists
content: There are seven villages in the area. A joint team of the army, police and the Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF) laid a tight cordon around the area and cleared four villages so far

title: Driven By Love, Divided By Border: PoK Man Who Crossed LoC For Lover Sent Back
content: The duo planned that Mir would cross the border, surrender directly to the army, and serve a legal sentence that was mandated for illegal entry into the country.

title: 'Thought People Hated Him': Trump On Iranians Crying At Khamenei's Funeral
content: Ali Khamenei, who ruled Iran for 36 years, was killed on February 28 - the day the US and Israel began striking Iran, triggering a war in the Middle East.

title: On Camera, Indonesian Troops Recover Body Of US Pilot Killed By Papua Rebels
content: Nicholas F Goselin, a pilot for Indonesian aviation company PT AMA, was shot dead Thursday shortly after he landed at the Ipdeheik airstrip in the mountainous province of Papua Highlands.

title: Crypto Horror In Gang-Rape Case Linked To Pakistan Deputy PM's Grandson
content: The allegations involve Raza Dar, who, the news agency PTI, citing police sources, said, is the grandson of Pakistan's Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar.

title: Iran Confirms Hormuz Transit Fees, Offers "Special Treatment" For "Friends"
content: The strait normally carries one-fifth of the world's crude oil and liquefied natural gas, but it was all but closed by Iran during the Middle East war, sending energy prices soaring.

title: "He Knows Who The Boss Is": Trump On Possible Netanyahu White House Visit
content: The possible meeting comes amid reports of tensions between the two leaders over efforts to end the war against Iran  that the US and Israel started in late February.

title: 'Stop the ads immediately': Centre's stern warning to Meta over child abuse content
content: Manash Pratim Gohain is a seasoned journalist with over two decades at The Times of India, where he has built a rich body of work spanning education policy, politics, and governance. Renowned for his incisive coverage of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, accreditation reforms, and skilling initiatives, he has also reported on student politics, urban policy, and social movements. His political reportage—both reflective and news-driven—adds depth to his writing, bridging policy with public impact. Through his 2,500 articles and related outlets, he has emerged as a trusted voice in national discourse, particularly in linking education reform to broader societal change. Read More
